query: What is the duration of Maternity Leave
eu_maternity_passage:
Duration of Maternity Leave EU law mandates a minimum of 14 weeks of maternity leave. 
Employers must allow at least 2 weeks before childbirth and 4 weeks after. However many EU 
countries provide longer maternity leave periods. For instance some countries offer up to a year or 
more. 
 
2. Maternity Pay EU legislation does not stipulate a minimum requirement for maternity pay but it 
requires that pay during maternity leave should be adequate to ensure the health and wellbeing of 
the mother and her child.
uk_maternity_passage:
UK Maternity cover policies  
 
1. Duration of Maternity Leave Eligible employees in the UK are entitled to up to 52 weeks of 
maternity leave. This is divided into two parts Ordinary Maternity Leave the first 26 weeks and 
Additional Maternity Leave the remaining 26 weeks. 
 
2. Maternity Pay Eligible employees can receive Statutory Maternity Pay SMP for up to 39 weeks.

GUIDANCE:
The following policy outlines the guidelines for maternity leave and pay in accordance with EU law and UK regulations.

Duration of Maternity Leave:
Employees are entitled to a minimum of 14 weeks of maternity leave as mandated by EU law, with at least 2 weeks before childbirth and 4 weeks after. However, the UK provides up to 52 weeks of maternity leave, divided into Ordinary Maternity Leave (26 weeks) and Additional Maternity Leave (26 weeks).

Maternity Pay:
EU legislation requires that pay during maternity leave should be adequate to ensure the health and wellbeing of the mother and her child. In the UK, eligible employees can receive Statutory Maternity Pay (SMP) for up to 39 weeks.

This policy combines the requirements of both EU law and UK regulations to ensure that employees receive adequate maternity leave and pay.

EU directives require that women on maternity leave must be entitled to return to the same job or an equivalent position with the same pay and benefits. Eligible employees in the UK are entitled to up to 52 weeks of maternity leave. This is divided into two parts Ordinary Maternity Leave - the first 26 weeks and Additional Maternity Leave - the remaining 26 weeks.

Eligible employees can receive Statutory Maternity Pay SMP for up to 39 weeks. SMP is paid at 90% of the employee's average weekly earnings for the first 6 weeks, followed by £151.97 per week or 90% of average weekly earnings, whichever is lower, for the remaining 33 weeks.

In addition, maternity pay EU legislation does not stipulate a minimum requirement for maternity pay but requires that pay during maternity leave should be adequate to ensure the health and well-being of the mother and her child.
